rulings.Case Title: GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI et al VS. PACIFIC BAY INVESTMENTS, INC., A CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF GIAMPAULO BOSCHETTI'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT. Granted in part, with 20 days leave to amend. The following language is stricken from the First Amended Cross-Complaint ("FACC"): (1) p. 14:3, the words "and for which Pacific Bay was entitled to commission"; (2) p. 14:14, the words "and under which Pacific Bay was entitled to commission" (3) p. 20:21, the words "and Pacific Bay;" (4) p. 24:23, the words "and Pacific Bay;" and (5) p. 26:27, the word "commissions." Cross-Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. The Court takes judicial notice of the fact that Pacific Bay Investments, Inc. is not licensed as a real estate broker in California, Texas or Hawaii. The FACC alleges that Pacific Bay Investments, Inc. is a distinct entity; it cannot collect commissions as a matter of law because it does not have a broker's license. Leave to amend is granted to permit Cross-Complainant Adam Sparks to assert claims for commission on his individual behalf, rather than on behalf of the corporation. (See In re Estate of Baldwin (1973) 34 Cal.App.3d 596, holding that the licensed broker can recover even though the contract provided that his corporation would be paid the commission.) The motion to strike is denied as to the allegations regarding Cross-Defendant Giampaolo Boschetti's percentage of the commission. The FACC alleges that Cross-Defendant is not entitled to the percentage because he did not earn it, not merely because he does not have a broker's license. (FACC, pars. 24-27.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: JOE DRUMMOND et al VS. FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA MUTUAL BENEFIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Quash Subpoenas Purpostedly Served On Canyon Pacific Management Co., Inc. And Eugene Burger Management Corporation
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 1, RESPONDENT FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION, Motion To Quash Subpoenas Purportedly Served On Canyon Pacific Management Co., Inc. And Eugene Burger Management Corporation. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The Court requires clarification as to the applicability to the pending motion to the earlier rulings of Judge Goldsmith and Judge Pro Tem Conor Moore. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LYNETTE ROSENBLUM VS. THOMAS EDWIN MCAVOY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Seeking Leave To Submit A Tardy Expert Witness List
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 3, DEFENDANT THOMAS MCAVOY, TRACY MCCLENDON Motion For Order Seeking Leave To Submit A Tardy Expert Witness List. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion by defendant to file a tardy Expert Witness List naming Paul Pershonock, M.D. as its retained expert is denied. It is procedurally improper for a party to first exchange an Expert Witness List representing "no expert testimony will be offered at trial", review the Expert Witness List exchanged by the adverse party, and then list an expert in a supplemental filing whose testimony is directed to an issue both sides had anticipated will be disputed at trial. See Fairfax v. Lords, 41 Cal. Rptr. 3d 850. The request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: APPBACKR INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION VS. MEHRAN EBADOLAHI et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Orer Transferring Venue To County Of Los Angeles Superior Court And For Order That Plaintiff Pay Deft'S Costs, Attorneys' Fees And Transfer Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $2,244.40 And Table Of Contents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, November 18, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT MEHRAN EBADOLAHI, SINA MOBASSER, BARMAX LLC, TESTMAX, INC.'S Motion for Order Transferring Venue To County Of Los Angeles Superior Court And For Order That Plaintiff Pay Defendant'S Costs, Attorneys' Fees And Transfer Fees And Costs In The Amount Of $2,244.40. The court denies the motion to change venue. Defendants have the burden of negating the propriety of venue as laid on all possible grounds. See Karson Industries, Inc. v. Sup. Crt. (1969) 273 Cal.App.2d 7, 9. Defendants fail to maintain that burden. Defendant BarMax LLC is the entity that entered into the contracts with plaintiff. BarMax LLC is a properly named party and represented by counsel. Defendants fail to show how venue is improper as to BarMax LLC. Defendants may have shown that TestMax, Inc., Mr. Ebadolahi, and Mr. Mobasser reside in Los Angeles. "[S]o long as the plaintiff chooses a venue that is proper as to one defendant, the entire case may be tried there, regardless of whether venue would be improper with respect to other defendants if the causes of action against them were analyzed separately." K.R.L. Partnership v. Sup. Crt. (2004) 120 Cal.App.4th 490, 504. The court denies plaintiff's request for judicial notice dated November 4, 2014 of the website. See Jolley v. Chase Home Finance, LLC (2013) 213 Cal.App.4th 872, 889. The Court grants plaintiff's supplemental request filed on November 12, 2014. All parties' requests for fees and costs in connection with this motion are denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)
